title: Multichain signature or quorum validation failure historical bridge incident

historical_exploit.attack_summary: Multichain's AnyswapV4Router contract suffered a rush attack, and the attacker made a profit of about 87 Ethereum, about $130,000. After analysis, the attacker used the MEV contract (0xd050) to pre-emptively call the anySwapOutUnderlyingWithPermit function of the AnyswapV4Router contract before the normal transaction was executed (the user authorized WETH but has not yet performed the transfer), although the function uses the permit signature of the token verification, but the stolen WETH this time does not have a relevant signature verification function, and only triggers a deposit function in a fallback. In subsequent function calls, the attacker can directly use the safeTransferFrom function to transfer the WETH authorized by the _underlying address to the attacked contract to the attack contract without signature verification.
historical_exploit.attacker_profile: External attacker or unprivileged caller as described by the SlowMist incident row; exact actor permissions must be re-verified before reporting.
preconditions: A bridge, cross-chain router, gateway, wrapped-asset minter, liquidity pool, or validator/proof path accepts attacker-controlled message, proof, signature, transaction, deposit, configuration, or accounting input.
The accepted input releases existing bridge-held value, mints unbacked wrapped assets, miscredits a deposit, or lets a caller move assets beyond entitlement.
The target mapping must be reachable without relying on privileged key compromise, DNS/frontend phishing, social-account compromise, or pure rug-pull behavior.

value_path.asset_or_reward: bridge-held assets, wrapped assets, bridge liquidity, withdrawals, refunds, or backed cross-chain liabilities
value_path.how_value_was_extracted: Multichain's AnyswapV4Router contract suffered a rush attack, and the attacker made a profit of about 87 Ethereum, about $130,000. After analysis, the attacker used the MEV contract (0xd050) to pre-emptively call the anySwapOutUnderlyingWithPermit function of the AnyswapV4Router contract before the normal transaction was executed (the user authorized WETH but has not yet performed the transfer), although the function uses the permit signature of the token verification, but the stolen WETH this time does not have a relevant signature verification function, and only triggers a deposit function in a fallback. In subsequent function calls, the attacker can directly use the safeTransferFrom function to transfer the WETH authorized by the _underlying address to the attacked contract to the attack contract without signature verification.
value_path.why_it_paid: The historical row describes real loss, attempted bridge theft, unbacked minting, unauthorized withdrawal, or bridge-accounting failure that can be useful only if BridgeIndex has a matching current live-value extraction path.

mapping.why_this_might_map: BridgeIndex exposes bridge deposits, bot-gas accounting, withdrawal creation/approval/execution, refunds, token/resource mappings, relayer voting, and proposal execution. This historical primitive is useful only if it maps to extracting current BNB/USDT ledger value or backed bridge liabilities from the deployed BridgeIndex system.

notes: Selected for scanned because: anySwapOutUnderlyingWithPermit rush/front-run attack
This seed is not proof that BridgeIndex is vulnerable; Workflow 7 must reject it unless the primitive maps to non-admin extraction of a current live ledger row.
